stem: A company wants to make user accounts harder to compromise by brute-force attacks.
model_answer: A brute-force attack tries many passwords (often from a list) against an account until one works. It can be slowed or stopped by: enforcing long, complex passwords that greatly increase the number of possibilities; limiting the number of failed login attempts before the account is locked; adding delays between attempts; using two-factor authentication so the password alone is not enough.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Explain how a brute-force attack works and give two ways to defend against it.

mark_points.text: the attacker tries many passwords until one succeeds
accepted_alternatives: guesses passwords by trying all combinations

mark_points.text: defence: enforce strong/long passwords to increase the search space
defence: limit the number of login attempts / lock the account after too many failures (or use two-factor authentication)
accepted_alternatives: use 2FA so the password alone is not enough
